prompt:
Take the FIRST provided reference image (v9 step 21 — dual-state composition with LEFT: hand lowering blood-filled vial into white protector tube body, ARROW, RIGHT: hand placing black cap onto assembled tube) and reproduce it PIXEL-FAITHFULLY in MOST elements — same dual-state composition, same hand poses, same vial appearance (transparent tube + red blood + plain ribbed cap), same arrow, same line-art style on pure white background, same line weight, same outlined line-art letter treatment, SAME TEXT ORIENTATION ON THE TUBE (first D of DIALED at the top of the cap reading top-down forward, first H of HEALTH at the top of the body reading top-down forward — DO NOT CHANGE this correctly-oriented text).

>>> THE ONLY FIX <<< — the PROTECTOR TUBE SHAPE/PROPORTIONS are wrong in v9. The body and cap are rendered too TALL and TOO NARROW (like test tubes / lipstick tubes). Restore the correct STUBBY PILL-BOTTLE proportions matching the SECOND reference image (the real product photo of the assembled protector tube — protector tube top.png):

  • Overall ASSEMBLED tube proportions: WIDER than it is tall, OR roughly as-wide-as-tall. Total assembled height ≈ 1.3–1.5x diameter. Think 'short stout pill bottle' or 'a fat chess pawn' — NOT a tall slim test tube.
  • WHITE BODY (lower section): a stubby cylinder. Body height ≈ slightly taller than its diameter (height ≈ 1.0–1.1x diameter on its own). DIAMETER IS WIDE — roughly two-thumb-widths in the hand's grip, NOT a slim one-finger pen.
  • BLACK CAP (upper section): same wide diameter as the body, cap height ≈ 0.5–0.6x its diameter (short squat cap). DIAMETER MATCHES the body's wide diameter.
  • Body-to-cap height ratio: roughly 60/40 (body slightly taller than the cap).

The text on the tube must STAY in the v9 correct orientation while the tube itself becomes wider/stubbier:
  • WHITE BODY in BOTH panels: 'HEALTH' rendered as a vertical column of 6 RIGHT-SIDE-UP outlined capital letters running top-down along the body's axis. First H at the top of the body. Last H at the bottom. Reading top-down: H, E, A, L, T, H forward. Outlined line-art letters (thin black strokes, white letter interiors).
  • BLACK CAP in the RIGHT panel: 'DIALED' rendered as a vertical column of 6 RIGHT-SIDE-UP outlined capital letters running top-down along the cap's axis. First D at the top of the cap. Last D at the bottom. Reading top-down: D, I, A, L, E, D forward. Outlined line-art letters (thin white strokes against black, black letter interiors).
  • Combined top-down reading of the assembled tube (RIGHT panel): 'DIALED HEALTH' linearly. This is exactly what v9 already shows for the text — preserve it.

The vial in the LEFT panel (transparent tube + red blood + ribbed cap) stays the same proportionally — still a tall narrow capped tube being lowered into the body's open mouth, even though the body itself is now wider and shorter.

Do NOT modify anything else — same dual-state composition, same hand poses, same vial appearance, same blood, same ribbed vial cap, same arrow, same line weight, same white background, same line-art aesthetic, same correctly-oriented text on the tube. The ONLY change is the tube proportions — make the body and cap STUBBY/WIDE matching the product photo silhouette.

No frame, no border, no other labels, no numbers.
refImages: DIaled Health Instruction Graphics/Product Refs/protector tube top.png
